---
title: "Página 2 — Calidad del Empleo"
subtitle: "EQUILAB · DAT4CCIÓN 2026"
date: today
lang: es
format:
  dashboard:
    theme: cosmo
    scrolling: true
    self-contained: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
library(arrow)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)

COLOR_HOMBRES <- "#2166AC"
COLOR_MUJERES <- "#D6604D"

df_ocup <- read_parquet("../datos/geih_ocupados_2019_2025.parquet")

posicion_labels <- c(
  "1" = "Empleado empresa particular",
  "2" = "Empleado gobierno",
  "3" = "Empleado doméstico",
  "4" = "Cuenta propia",
  "5" = "Empleador",
  "6" = "Familiar sin remuneración",
  "7" = "Sin remuneración (otras emp.)",
  "8" = "Jornalero / peón",
  "9" = "Otro"
)

df_base <- df_ocup |>
  filter(
    condicion_activ == 1,
    edad >= 15, edad <= 99,
    sexo %in% c(1, 2)
  ) |>
  mutate(
    sexo_label     = if_else(sexo == 1, "Hombres", "Mujeres"),
    posicion_label = posicion_labels[as.character(posicion_ocup)]
  )

data_plot <- df_base |>
  group_by(anio, posicion_label, sexo_label) |>
  summarise(n_pond = sum(fex, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(anio, posicion_label) |>
  mutate(pct = n_pond / sum(n_pond) * 100) |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(!is.na(posicion_label))
```

## Row

### Proporción por sexo y categoría ocupacional (2019–2025) {width="100%"}

```{r viz-heatmap}
# Datos: % mujeres + población total estimada por categoría y año
heatmap_data <- data_plot |>
  group_by(anio, posicion_label) |>
  mutate(n_total = sum(n_pond)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(sexo_label == "Mujeres") |>
  mutate(n_mill = round(n_total / 1e6, 2)) |>
  select(anio, posicion_label, pct, n_mill)

# Ordenar categorías de más feminizada a más masculinizada (promedio histórico)
orden_cats <- heatmap_data |>
  group_by(posicion_label) |>
  summarise(pct_promedio = mean(pct, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  arrange(desc(pct_promedio)) |>
  pull(posicion_label)

heatmap_data <- heatmap_data |>
  mutate(posicion_label = factor(posicion_label, levels = rev(orden_cats)))

p_heat <- ggplot(heatmap_data,
    aes(x = factor(anio), y = posicion_label,
        fill = pct,
        text = paste0(
          "<b>", posicion_label, "</b><br>",
          "Año: ", anio, "<br>",
          "% mujeres: ", round(pct, 1), "%<br>",
          "Personas aprox.: ", format(n_mill, nsmall = 2), " millones"
        ))) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(pct, 0), "%")), size = 3, color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low  = COLOR_HOMBRES,
    mid  = "#f7f7f7",
    high = COLOR_MUJERES,
    midpoint = 50,
    limits = c(0, 100),
    name = "% Mujeres"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Composición por sexo según tipo de empleo — Colombia 2019–2025",
    x = NULL, y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
  )

ggplotly(p_heat, tooltip = "text") |>
  layout(
    margin = list(b = 60),
    annotations = list(list(
      x = 0, y = -0.12, xref = "paper", yref = "paper",
      text = "Fuente: DANE, GEIH 2019–2025. Cálculos: EQUILAB — DAT4CCIÓN 2026.",
      showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "left",
      font = list(size = 9, color = "gray50")
    ))
  )
```

## Row

### Horas trabajadas promedio por tipo de empleo y sexo {width="100%"}

```{r viz-horas}
# Media ponderada de horas por posición ocupacional y sexo — año más reciente
anio_ref <- max(df_base$anio)

horas_cat <- df_base |>
  filter(anio == anio_ref, !is.na(horas_sem), horas_sem > 0,
         !is.na(posicion_label)) |>
  group_by(posicion_label, sexo_label) |>
  summarise(
    horas_prom = weighted.mean(horas_sem, w = fex, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_mill     = sum(fex, na.rm = TRUE) / 1e6,
    .groups    = "drop"
  )

# Ordenar categorías por promedio de hombres (de mayor a menor arriba)
orden_cats <- horas_cat |>
  filter(sexo_label == "Hombres") |>
  arrange(horas_prom) |>
  pull(posicion_label)

horas_cat <- horas_cat |>
  mutate(posicion_label = factor(posicion_label, levels = orden_cats))

x_max <- ceiling(max(horas_cat$horas_prom)) + 8

plot_ly(
  horas_cat,
  x             = ~horas_prom,
  y             = ~posicion_label,
  color         = ~sexo_label,
  colors        = c("Hombres" = COLOR_HOMBRES, "Mujeres" = COLOR_MUJERES),
  type          = "bar",
  orientation   = "h",
  text          = ~paste0(round(horas_prom, 1), "h"),
  textposition  = "outside",
  textfont      = list(size = 11, color = "gray30"),
  hovertemplate = ~paste0(
    "<b>", posicion_label, "</b><br>",
    sexo_label, ": ", round(horas_prom, 1), " h/semana<br>",
    round(n_mill, 2), " millones de personas",
    "<extra></extra>"
  )
) |>
  layout(
    barmode = "group",
    bargap  = 0.25,
    title   = list(
      text = paste0("Horas trabajadas por semana según tipo de empleo — Colombia ", anio_ref),
      font = list(size = 13), x = 0
    ),
    xaxis = list(
      title    = "Horas trabajadas por semana (promedio ponderado)",
      range    = c(0, x_max),
      zeroline = FALSE,
      showgrid = TRUE,
      gridcolor = "#f0f0f0"
    ),
    yaxis  = list(title = ""),
    legend = list(orientation = "h", x = 0.5, xanchor = "center", y = 1.08),
    plot_bgcolor  = "white",
    paper_bgcolor = "white",
    annotations   = list(list(
      x = 1, y = -0.08, xref = "paper", yref = "paper",
      text = paste0("Fuente: DANE, GEIH ", anio_ref, ". Cálculos: EQUILAB — DAT4CCIÓN 2026."),
      showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "right",
      font = list(size = 9, color = "gray50")
    )),
    margin = list(l = 170, r = 80, t = 50, b = 50)
  )
```

## Row

### ¿Cuántas personas trabajan en cada categoría? {width="100%"}

```{r viz-horas-tamano}
# Composición por sexo (%) — mismo año y mismo orden que la gráfica de horas
composicion_cat <- df_base |>
  filter(anio == anio_ref, !is.na(posicion_label)) |>
  group_by(posicion_label, sexo_label) |>
  summarise(n_pond = sum(fex, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(posicion_label) |>
  mutate(
    pct    = n_pond / sum(n_pond) * 100,
    n_mill = round(n_pond / 1e6, 2)
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(posicion_label = factor(posicion_label, levels = orden_cats))

plot_ly(
  composicion_cat,
  x             = ~posicion_label,
  y             = ~pct,
  color         = ~sexo_label,
  colors        = c("Hombres" = COLOR_HOMBRES, "Mujeres" = COLOR_MUJERES),
  type          = "bar",
  text          = ~paste0(round(pct, 0), "%"),
  textposition  = "inside",
  insidetextanchor = "middle",
  textfont      = list(size = 11, color = "white", family = "Arial Bold"),
  hovertemplate = ~paste0(
    "<b>", posicion_label, "</b><br>",
    sexo_label, ": ", round(pct, 1), "%<br>",
    round(n_mill, 2), " millones de personas",
    "<extra></extra>"
  )
) |>
  layout(
    barmode       = "stack",
    bargap        = 0.3,
    title         = list(
      text = paste0("Composición por sexo según tipo de empleo — Colombia ", anio_ref),
      font = list(size = 13), x = 0
    ),
    xaxis = list(
      title     = "",
      tickangle = -30,
      tickfont  = list(size = 10)
    ),
    yaxis = list(
      title      = "% de trabajadores",
      ticksuffix = "%",
      range      = c(0, 115)   # espacio extra arriba para que la leyenda no tape las barras
    ),
    legend        = list(orientation = "h", x = 0.5, xanchor = "center", y = 1.18),
    plot_bgcolor  = "white",
    paper_bgcolor = "white",
    annotations   = list(list(
      x = 1, y = -0.38, xref = "paper", yref = "paper",
      text = paste0("Fuente: DANE, GEIH ", anio_ref, ". Cálculos: EQUILAB — DAT4CCIÓN 2026."),
      showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "right",
      font = list(size = 9, color = "gray50")
    )),
    margin = list(l = 60, r = 20, t = 70, b = 130)
  )
```

## Row

### Informalidad laboral por sexo — Colombia 2019–2025 {width="100%"}

```{r viz-informalidad}
# Total informal por sexo y año (proxy corregido 4+6+7+8)
df_inf_linea <- df_base |>
  mutate(informal = posicion_ocup %in% c(4, 6, 7, 8)) |>
  group_by(anio, sexo_label) |>
  summarise(
    pct_inf = sum(fex[informal], na.rm = TRUE) / sum(fex, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    n_mill  = sum(fex[informal], na.rm = TRUE) / 1e6,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

h_l <- df_inf_linea |> filter(sexo_label == "Hombres") |> arrange(anio)
m_l <- df_inf_linea |> filter(sexo_label == "Mujeres")  |> arrange(anio)
y_max_l <- ceiling(max(df_inf_linea$pct_inf)) + 5

plot_ly() |>
  add_polygons(
    x = c(h_l$anio, rev(m_l$anio)),
    y = c(h_l$pct_inf, rev(m_l$pct_inf)),
    fillcolor = "#d1d9e6", opacity = 0.45,
    line = list(color = "transparent"),
    showlegend = FALSE, hoverinfo = "none"
  ) |>
  add_lines(
    data = h_l, x = ~anio, y = ~pct_inf, name = "Hombres",
    line = list(color = COLOR_HOMBRES, width = 2.5),
    mode = "lines+markers",
    marker = list(color = COLOR_HOMBRES, size = 9,
                  line = list(color = "white", width = 1.5)),
    text = ~paste0("<b>Hombres</b><br>Año: ", anio, "<br>",
                   round(pct_inf, 1), "% informales<br>",
                   round(n_mill, 2), " millones"),
    hovertemplate = "%{text}<extra></extra>"
  ) |>
  add_lines(
    data = m_l, x = ~anio, y = ~pct_inf, name = "Mujeres",
    line = list(color = COLOR_MUJERES, width = 2.5),
    mode = "lines+markers",
    marker = list(color = COLOR_MUJERES, size = 9,
                  line = list(color = "white", width = 1.5)),
    text = ~paste0("<b>Mujeres</b><br>Año: ", anio, "<br>",
                   round(pct_inf, 1), "% informales<br>",
                   round(n_mill, 2), " millones"),
    hovertemplate = "%{text}<extra></extra>"
  ) |>
  layout(
    title  = list(text = "Proxy de informalidad laboral por sexo — Colombia 2019–2025",
                  font = list(size = 13), x = 0),
    xaxis  = list(title = "", tickvals = 2019:2025,
                  ticktext = as.character(2019:2025), zeroline = FALSE),
    yaxis  = list(title = "% del total de ocupados", ticksuffix = "%",
                  range = c(0, y_max_l)),
    legend = list(orientation = "h", x = 0.5, xanchor = "center", y = 1.08),
    shapes = list(list(type = "line", x0 = 2021, x1 = 2021,
                       y0 = 0, y1 = 1, yref = "paper",
                       line = list(color = "gray60", dash = "dot", width = 1.2))),
    annotations = list(
      list(x = 2021, y = y_max_l - 2, xref = "x", yref = "y",
           text = "Cambio<br>Marco 2021", showarrow = FALSE,
           xanchor = "left", xshift = 5,
           font = list(size = 8, color = "gray50")),
      list(x = 0.99, y = 0.97, xref = "paper", yref = "paper",
           text = "<b>Proxy incluye:</b><br>· Cuenta propia<br>· Familiar sin remuneración<br>· Jornalero / peón",
           showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "right", yanchor = "top", align = "left",
           bgcolor = "#f8f9fa", bordercolor = "gray80", borderwidth = 1,
           font = list(size = 9, color = "gray40")),
      list(x = 1, y = -0.1, xref = "paper", yref = "paper",
           text = "Fuente: DANE, GEIH 2019–2025. Cálculos: EQUILAB — DAT4CCIÓN 2026.",
           showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "right",
           font = list(size = 9, color = "gray50"))
    ),
    plot_bgcolor = "white", paper_bgcolor = "white",
    margin = list(t = 60, b = 60, l = 60, r = 20)
  )
```

## Row

### Segregación sectorial por sexo — Colombia 2025 {width="100%"}

```{r viz-segregacion}
# Función para agrupar código CIIU 2 dígitos en secciones
seccion_ciiu <- function(cod) {
  cod <- as.integer(cod)
  dplyr::case_when(
    cod %in% 1:3   ~ "Agricultura y pesca",
    cod %in% 5:9   ~ "Minas y canteras",
    cod %in% 10:33 ~ "Industria manufacturera",
    cod == 35       ~ "Electricidad y gas",
    cod %in% 36:39 ~ "Agua y saneamiento",
    cod %in% 41:43 ~ "Construcción",
    cod %in% 45:47 ~ "Comercio",
    cod %in% 49:53 ~ "Transporte y almacenamiento",
    cod %in% 55:56 ~ "Alojamiento y comidas",
    cod %in% 58:63 ~ "Información y comunicaciones",
    cod %in% 64:66 ~ "Financiero y seguros",
    cod == 68       ~ "Actividades inmobiliarias",
    cod %in% 69:75 ~ "Profesionales y científicas",
    cod %in% 77:82 ~ "Servicios administrativos",
    cod == 84       ~ "Administración pública",
    cod == 85       ~ "Educación",
    cod %in% 86:88 ~ "Salud y asistencia social",
    cod %in% 90:93 ~ "Arte y entretenimiento",
    cod %in% 94:96 ~ "Otros servicios",
    cod == 97       ~ "Hogares como empleadores",
    TRUE            ~ NA_character_
  )
}

anio_ref_seg <- max(df_base$anio)

# Composición por sexo y sección — año más reciente
seg_data <- df_base |>
  filter(anio == anio_ref_seg, !is.na(rama2d_orig)) |>
  mutate(seccion = seccion_ciiu(rama2d_orig)) |>
  filter(!is.na(seccion)) |>
  group_by(seccion, sexo_label) |>
  summarise(pob = sum(fex, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(seccion) |>
  mutate(
    total  = sum(pob),
    pct_m  = pob[sexo_label == "Mujeres"] / total * 100
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(sexo_label == "Mujeres") |>
  mutate(
    total_mill = round(total / 1e6, 1),
    pct_m      = round(pct_m, 1),
    color_bar  = if_else(pct_m >= 50, COLOR_MUJERES, COLOR_HOMBRES)
  ) |>
  arrange(pct_m) |>
  mutate(seccion = factor(seccion, levels = seccion))

# Índice de Duncan — año más reciente
duncan_val <- df_base |>
  filter(anio == anio_ref_seg, !is.na(rama2d_orig)) |>
  mutate(seccion = seccion_ciiu(rama2d_orig)) |>
  filter(!is.na(seccion)) |>
  group_by(seccion, sexo_label) |>
  summarise(pob = sum(fex, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(sexo_label) |>
  mutate(share = pob / sum(pob)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(seccion, sexo_label, share) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = sexo_label, values_from = share, values_fill = 0) |>
  summarise(duncan = round(0.5 * sum(abs(Mujeres - Hombres)) * 100, 1)) |>
  pull(duncan)

n_sec <- nrow(seg_data)

plot_ly(
  seg_data,
  x             = ~pct_m,
  y             = ~seccion,
  type          = "bar",
  orientation   = "h",
  marker        = list(color = ~color_bar),
  hovertemplate = ~paste0(
    "<b>", seccion, "</b><br>",
    round(pct_m, 1), "% mujeres<br>",
    total_mill, " millones de ocupados",
    "<extra></extra>"
  ),
  showlegend = FALSE
) |>
  layout(
    title  = list(
      text = paste0("Segregación sectorial por sexo — Colombia ", anio_ref_seg),
      font = list(size = 13), x = 0
    ),
    xaxis = list(
      title      = "% de mujeres en el sector",
      ticksuffix = "%",
      range      = c(0, 108),
      zeroline   = FALSE,
      showgrid   = TRUE,
      gridcolor  = "#f0f0f0"
    ),
    yaxis = list(title = ""),
    shapes = list(list(
      type = "line",
      x0 = 50, x1 = 50, y0 = -0.5, y1 = n_sec - 0.5,
      xref = "x", yref = "y",
      line = list(color = "gray50", dash = "dot", width = 1.2)
    )),
    annotations = list(
      list(
        x = 50, y = n_sec - 0.5, xref = "x", yref = "y",
        text = "50%", showarrow = FALSE,
        xanchor = "left", xshift = 4,
        font = list(size = 9, color = "gray50")
      ),
      list(
        x = 0.99, y = 0.97, xref = "paper", yref = "paper",
        text = paste0(
          "<b>Índice de Duncan ", anio_ref_seg, ": ", duncan_val, "%</b><br>",
          "El ", duncan_val, "% de las mujeres ocupadas<br>",
          "tendría que cambiar de sector para<br>",
          "igualar la distribución de los hombres.<br>",
          "<span style='color:gray'>(2019: 34.4% · ↑ tras la pandemia)</span>"
        ),
        showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "right", yanchor = "top", align = "left",
        bgcolor = "#f8f9fa", bordercolor = "gray80", borderwidth = 1,
        font = list(size = 9, color = "gray40")
      ),
      list(
        x = 1, y = -0.05, xref = "paper", yref = "paper",
        text = paste0("Fuente: DANE, GEIH ", anio_ref_seg,
                      ". Cálculos: EQUILAB — DAT4CCIÓN 2026."),
        showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "right",
        font = list(size = 9, color = "gray50")
      )
    ),
    plot_bgcolor  = "white",
    paper_bgcolor = "white",
    margin = list(l = 220, r = 20, t = 60, b = 60)
  )
```
